description_long: This is a large, bulbous ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body tapering to a narrower base and a tall, cylindrical neck. The surface is matte and unglazed, exhibiting a natural grayish-brown clay color with subtle variations and some visible wear. A single, sturdy handle is attached from the upper neck to the shoulder, featuring slight ridges and a hand-formed appearance. The rim of the vase is uneven and slightly flared, suggesting it was crafted by hand rather than machine. There are no visible decorative motifs or painted patterns, giving the vase a utilitarian and rustic aesthetic. The overall form and finish suggest it could be an antique or traditional vessel, suitable for display in outdoor or urban heritage settings.
description: A large, rustic gray ceramic vase with a wide body, tall neck, and a single sturdy handle. The surface is matte and unadorned, showing natural wear and hand-crafted details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide body, tall neck, and the handle attached to the right side, with the rim and opening clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the full curve of the handle and the smooth, rounded back of the vase.
description_view_2: Side view with the handle on the left, showing the vase's broadest profile and the subtle surface texture.
description_view_3: View from the opposite side of the handle, focusing on the neck and rim, with the handle mostly hidden.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Virtual dos Louceiros de Bamio
